disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Both legs turn dark purple/blue and throb when standing or when the legs hang down
trigger_question_examples: What happens to your legs when you stand?
Tell me about the colour change and the pain.
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: When I stand or let my legs hang down, both of them go dark purple and throb and ache — really deep and uncomfortable.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The leg discolouration and pain resolve within seconds of elevating the legs or lying flat
trigger_question_examples: What happens to the colour when you lift your legs up or lie down?
Does raising your legs make the colour and pain go away?
How quickly does it settle once your legs are up?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: As soon as I lift my legs up or lie flat the purple goes back to normal within a few seconds and the throbbing eases. It's only when they're hanging down.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: It just kind of changes, I haven't really timed it.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Heart pounds and races, vision tunnels and blacks out with dizziness within seconds of standing
trigger_question_examples: What happens to your heart and your vision when you stand?
Do you feel dizzy or faint when upright?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My heart pounds and races as soon as I'm up, my vision goes all tunnel and blacks out, and I get really dizzy like I'll faint.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Both legs are affected equally and symmetrically; no single leg has become fixed pale, cold, numb or pulseless
trigger_question_examples: Is it both legs equally, or is one leg worse than the other?
Has either leg gone pale, white, cold or numb and stayed that way?
Is one calf swollen, hot or tender compared with the other?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's both legs the same — they both go purple together and both go back to normal together. Neither one has gone white or cold or numb, and they're not swollen or hot, it's not one worse than the other.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I think they're both about the same, I haven't looked that closely.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No chest pain, and no racing/palpitations when lying still
trigger_question_examples: Do you get any chest pain?
Does your heart race or thump when you're lying still and resting?
Any palpitations at rest?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No chest pain at all. When I'm lying still my heart feels normal — it's only when I stand up that it races.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I haven't really noticed it at rest.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Longstanding known postural intolerance under specialist investigation, now in a severe flare, with attempts to keep salt and fluids up
trigger_question_examples: Has this been a longstanding problem?
Is anyone already looking into this for you?
What have you been doing to manage it?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've had the racing heart and faintness on standing for a long time and my specialist is investigating why my body doesn't control the blood flow to my legs. I try to keep my salt and fluids up like I was told, but this flare has really overwhelmed me.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Still able to sip fluids and passing urine; no persistent vomiting
trigger_question_examples: Are you able to keep fluids down?
Are you still passing urine normally?
Have you been vomiting?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I can sip my salty electrolyte drinks and I'm still weeing, and I'm not vomiting.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Near-faints on standing but no actual loss of consciousness and no fall/injury
trigger_question_examples: Have you actually passed out or blacked out completely?
Have you fallen or hurt yourself?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I keep nearly fainting but I haven't actually passed out — I lie down or crawl before I go down. I haven't fallen or hit my head.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I felt like I'd faint but I'm not sure exactly what happened.
